title: Build Mission Control
description: Build the Mission Control Next.js app with 6 tabs: Tasks, Calendar, Memory, Documents, People, Office
why: Central hub to track all projects, agent activity, and memory across our growing team

title: Deploy Mission Control to Vercel
description: Deploy to Vercel and connect to GitHub (zhunton/mission-control)
why: Make Mission Control accessible from any device, not just the Mac Mini

title: UI Improvements Round 1
description: Task detail modals, column color coding, calendar cleanup, memory restructure, larger org chart
why: Polish the UI based on Zach's initial feedback

title: Pixel Art Avatars & Avatar Fix
description: Integrate Wally (wizard) and Zach (king) pixel art avatars across Office, People, and sidebar. Fix broken rendering with onError handlers.
why: Visual identity for agents — medieval theme consistency across Mission Control

title: Add Patch & Dali to Mission Control
description: Add Patch (Software Developer) and Dali (Image Developer) to org chart, People page, Office canvas, and Tasks. Fix role labels and profile pic rendering.
why: Active agents should be visible and tracked in Mission Control

title: Canvas Office Room — Full Rebuild
description: Replace background image + floating avatars with a fully coded canvas room. Dedicated workstations per agent, pathfinding, AI-generated pixel art sprites from Dali, slow walking behavior.
why: Zach wants a real game-style environment, not a UI with a photo background. Castle aesthetic with actual agent characters walking between zones.

title: Task Persistence — JSON File Storage
description: Replace in-memory React state with persistent JSON file storage (/data/tasks.json). API routes for GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE. Protection rule: Zach-owned and backlog tasks cannot be deleted by syncs.
why: Zach's manually-added backlog tasks were being wiped on every new build. Tasks need to survive deploys, syncs, and tab closes.

title: Remove Filler Agents from Org Chart
description: Remove Research Agent, Dev Agent, and Writing Agent from the People page. Active team only: Zach, Wally, Patch, Dali.
why: Placeholder agents with no real work create noise. Only show active agents.

title: Build Coaching Tab
description: New Coaching tab in Mission Control with quiz session tracking, performance chart, category breakdowns, and session history with expandable question review.
why: Track Zach's daily quiz performance, category strengths/weaknesses, and learning progress over time

title: Documents Tab — Markdown, Download & PDF
description: Proper markdown rendering, .txt download, and PDF generation (via print dialog) added to Documents tab. Hunton genealogy document updated with accurate Compendium text.
why: Documents tab needed polish to be actually useful — raw text wasn't readable and there was no way to save a doc.

title: Office Page Overhaul v2
description: Review and discuss the Sandler Selling System — a consultative sales methodology focused on qualifying prospects, controlling the conversation, and reversing traditional sales dynamics. Context: Hunton Group has used Sandler historically across all companies. HS is doing a refresh now because the company has grown. This is for HS's dedicated sales team (not techs or ASMs), who sell service contracts and project work. Goal is to make the sales process more efficient and controlled.
why: The Sandler refresh is happening at HS now. As Zach learns the business, understanding the sales methodology the team is trained on — why it works, how it applies to HS's specific selling environment — helps him lead and evaluate the sales organization more effectively.

title: Add Atlas Research Agent
description: Atlas added to org chart (People page) with profile photo and placed in the Alchemy Room on the Office canvas.
why: Research Agent role — placeholder until Perplexity or similar is wired in.

title: Build Idea Lab Tab
description: New Idea Lab tab in Mission Control for capturing and developing ideas.
why: Structured place to hold ideas before they become tasks.

title: Mission Control — ongoing improvements
description: Continuous development and improvement of Mission Control features
why: Patch owns the technical build of Mission Control. As new features are needed, Patch implements them under Wally's direction.

title: Review Karpathy Tweet — AI & Programming Refactor
description: Andrej Karpathy posted that he's never felt more behind as a programmer due to AI's rapid pace. He says the profession is being 'dramatically refactored' and that he could be 10x more powerful if he properly strung together tools available over the last year. Worth reviewing and discussing implications.
why: Karpathy is one of the sharpest minds in AI. When he says programmers are falling behind their own tools, it's a signal worth paying attention to — both for how we operate and for the side income ideas we're exploring.

title: Discuss: Satellite Imagery HVAC Lead Detection Tool
description: Explore building a software tool that uses satellite imagery and Google Maps API to detect cooling towers, RTUs, and commercial HVAC equipment on building rooftops. The tool would identify buildings with aging or detectable equipment and generate qualified leads for HVAC service companies — particularly Hunton Group.
why: Hunton Services and Hunton Trane both need a steady pipeline of commercial leads. A satellite-based detection tool could identify buildings with equipment that needs service or replacement before a competitor does. First-mover advantage in a building's service contract is everything in this industry. If this works, it could also become a standalone product sold to other HVAC companies.

title: Discuss: Sandler Selling System
description: Review and discuss the Sandler Selling System — a consultative sales methodology focused on qualifying prospects, uncovering pain, and reversing traditional sales dynamics. Explore how it applies to Hunton Group's sales process across HT, HS, and CVZ.
why: Zach is learning the business top to bottom with a path to CEO. Understanding proven sales methodologies — and how they apply to Hunton's complex B2B sales cycles — is critical for both personal development and improving company performance.
